input.command:
echo "=== pods ==="
kubectl -n cockroachdb get pods -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.metadata.name}: {.spec.containers[0].image}{"\n"}{end}'
echo "=== cluster setting version ==="
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec crdb-cluster-0 -- ./cockroach sql --insecure -e "SHOW CLUSTER SETTING version;"
echo "=== preserve_downgrade_option ==="
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec crdb-cluster-0 -- ./cockroach sql --insecure -e "SHOW CLUSTER SETTING cluster.preserve_downgrade_option;"
echo "=== sts partition ==="
kubectl -n cockroachdb get sts crdb-cluster -o jsonpath='{.spec.updateStrategy.rollingUpdate.partition}'
echo
echo "=== node status ==="
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec crdb-cluster-0 -- ./cockroach node status --insecure

content.text: I'll follow CockroachDB's standard major-upgrade procedure: set `preserve_downgrade_option` first (so the cluster doesn't auto-finalize mid-rollout), roll the binaries to v24.2.0 one pod at a time with health checks between each, then finalize.

input.command:
# Step 1: prevent auto-finalization during rollout, preserving ability to downgrade if something goes wrong
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec crdb-cluster-0 -- ./cockroach sql --insecure -e "SET CLUSTER SETTING cluster.preserve_downgrade_option = '24.1';"
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec crdb-cluster-0 -- ./cockroach sql --insecure -e "SHOW CLUSTER SETTING cluster.preserve_downgrade_option;"

description:

kubectl -n cockroachdb exec crdb-cluster-0 -- ./cockroach sql --insecure -e "SET CLUSTER SETTING cluster.preserve_downgrade_option = '24.1';"
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec crdb-cluster-0 -- ./cockroach sql --insecure -e "SHOW CLUSTER SETTING cluster.preserve_downgrade_option;"

summary:
# Step 1: prevent auto-finalization during rollout, preserving ability to downgrade if something goes wrong
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec crdb-cluster-0 -- ./cockroach sql --insecure -e "SET CLUSTER SETTING cluster.preserve_downgrade_option = '24.1';"
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec crdb-cluster-0 -- ./cockroach sql --insecure -e "SHOW CLUSTER SETTING cluster.preserve_downgrade_option;"

content.text: Now roll out the v24.2.0 binary, one pod at a time, using staged partitions like the previous minor upgrade.

input.command:
kubectl -n cockroachdb patch sts crdb-cluster --type merge -p '{"spec":{"updateStrategy":{"rollingUpdate":{"partition":2}}}}'
kubectl -n cockroachdb set image sts/crdb-cluster db=cockroachdb/cockroach:v24.2.0
kubectl -n cockroachdb rollout status sts/crdb-cluster --timeout=180s 2>&1 | tail -20
kubectl -n cockroachdb get pods -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.metadata.name}: {.spec.containers[0].image}{"\n"}{end}'

description:
kubectl -n cockroachdb patch sts crdb-cluster --type merge -p '{"spec":{"updateStrategy":{"rollingUpdate":{"partition":2}}}}'
kubectl -n cockroachdb set image sts/crdb-cluster db=cockroachdb/cockroach:v24.2.0
kubectl -n cockroachdb rollout status sts/crdb-cluster --timeout=180s 2>&1 | tail -20
kubectl -n cockroachdb get pods -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.metadata.name}: {.spec.containers[0].image}{"\n"}{end}'
